text.title: test-golden-master-legacy-rewrite Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for test-golden-master-legacy-rewrite. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Before any agent touches the legacy module, capture at least 200 distinct (input, expected_output) pairs into a versioned JSONL corpus under tests/golden/. The capture script MUST normalize timestamps (frozen value), random IDs (deterministic seed or strip), and order-sensitive collections (sort by stable key). The corpus file MUST be committed and immutable; new pairs append, existing pairs MUST NOT be silently rewritten.
rule.rationale: Untested legacy has no other spec. Without a captured baseline, every agent rewrite is unverifiable. Without normalization, the replay test goes flaky and gets disabled — the worst possible outcome.

rule.statement: The replay test MUST iterate the entire corpus and assert byte-for-byte parity (after the same normalization used at capture). On divergence the test MUST print case_id, input, and a unified diff of expected vs actual. CI MUST fail when any case diverges. Updating an expected value requires a separate commit whose message starts with golden: and explains the behaviour change; PR review checks each updated row.
rule.rationale: Bulk-accepting a corpus diff turns golden-master into a rubber-stamp. Per-row review keeps the cost of "I changed behaviour" visible to the human approver and forces the agent to surface its assumptions.
